---
name: load-unpacked-extension
description: 'Launch a Chromium browser instance via Playwright/Puppeteer with the local unpacked extension loaded for E2E testing.'
argument-hint: 'Use when you need to run E2E tests or interact with the extension UI / content scripts.'
---

# Extension Loader Skill for Browser Testing

Use this skill when you need to automatically or manually verify Chrome Extension behavior (Popups, Content Scripts, Background processing) in a real browser.

## Why This Skill Exists
Standard browser automation opens an empty, stateless browser. To test a Chrome Extension, you must pass specific command-line arguments to Chromium to load the unpacked directory generated by your build step.

## Procedure for Playwright / Puppeteer

1. Ensure the extension is built (e.g., `pnpm build`) and locate the output directory (usually `./dist`).
2. Construct the absolute path to the extension directory.
3. Launch the browser using the `--disable-extensions-except` and `--load-extension` flags.

### Example Playwright Snippet

```javascript
import { chromium } from 'playwright';
import path from 'path';

const pathToExtension = path.join(process.cwd(), 'dist'); // Adjust if output folder differs

const userDataDir = '/tmp/test-user-data-dir';

// You MUST use launchPersistentContext to load extensions in Playwright
const browserContext = await chromium.launchPersistentContext(userDataDir, {
  headless: false, // Chrome extensions often require headed mode to test UI properly
  args: [
    `--disable-extensions-except=${pathToExtension}`,
    `--load-extension=${pathToExtension}`
  ]
});

// Navigate to a target page to test content scripts
const page = await browserContext.newPage();
await page.goto('[https://example.com](https://example.com)'); // Replace with URL that triggers your content script

// To test the Popup, you will need to navigate to its internal URL:
// chrome-extension://<EXTENSION_ID>/popup.html
